oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000001-WSR-000002
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The IIS 10.0 website session state must be enabled.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
Note: If ASP.NET is not installed, this is Not Applicable.
Follow the procedures below for each site hosted on the IIS 10.0 web server:
Open the IIS 10.0 Manager.
Click the site name.
Under the "ASP.NET" section, select "Session State".
Under "Session State Mode Settings", verify the "In Process" mode is selected.
If the "Session State Mode Settings" is set to "In Process", this is not a finding.
Alternative method:
Click the site name.
Select "Configuration Editor" under the "Management" section.
From the "Section:" drop-down list at the top of the configuration editor, locate "system.web/sessionState".
Verify the "mode" reflects "InProc".
If the "mode" is not set to "InProc", this is a finding.
If the system being reviewed is part of a Web Farm, interview the System Administrator to ensure Session State Tracking is enabled via a SQL server, or other means. If Session State Tracking is enabled on the Web Farm, this is not a finding.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000001-WSR-000002
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The IIS 10.0 website session state cookie settings must be configured to Use Cookies mode.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
Follow the procedures below for each site hosted on the IIS 10.0 web server: Open the IIS 10.0 Manager. Click the site name. Under the "ASP.NET" section, select "Session State". Under "Cookie Settings", verify the "Use Cookies" mode is selected from the "Mode:" drop-down list. If the "Use Cookies" mode is selected, this is not a finding.
Alternative method: Click the site name. Select "Configuration Editor" under the "Management" section. From the "Section:" drop-down list at the top of the configuration editor, locate "system.web/sessionState". Verify the "cookieless" is set to "UseCookies". If the "cookieless" is not set to "UseCookies", this is a finding. Note: If IIS 10.0 server/site is used only for system-to-system maintenance, does not allow users to connect to interface, and is restricted to specific system IPs, this is Not Applicable.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000014-WSR-000006
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: A private IIS 10.0 website must only accept Secure Socket Layer (SSL) connections.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Note: If the server being reviewed is a public IIS 10.0 web server, this is Not Applicable. Note: If the server is hosting SharePoint, this is Not Applicable. Note: If the server is hosting WSUS, this is Not Applicable. Note: If SSL is installed on load balancer/proxy server through which traffic is routed to the IIS 10.0 server, and the IIS 10.0 server receives traffic from the load balancer/proxy server, the SSL requirement must be met on the load balancer/proxy server and is Not Applicable in this STIG. Follow the procedures below for each site hosted on the IIS 10.0 web server: Open the IIS 10.0 Manager. Click the site name. Double-click the 'SSL Settings' icon. Verify 'Require SSL' check box is selected. If the 'Require SSL' check box is not selected, this is a finding.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000014-WSR-000006
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: A public IIS 10.0 website must only accept Secure Socket Layer (SSL) connections when authentication is required.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Note: If the server being reviewed is a private IIS 10.0 web server, this is Not Applicable. Note: If the server being reviewed is a public IIS 10.0 web server not requiring authentication, this is Not Applicable. Note: If the server being reviewed is hosting SharePoint, this is Not Applicable. Note: If the server being reviewed is hosting WSUS, this is Not Applicable. Follow the procedures below for each site hosted on the IIS 10.0 web server: Open the IIS 10.0 Manager. Click the site name. Double-click the 'SSL Settings' icon. Verify 'Require SSL' check box is selected. If the 'Require SSL' check box is not selected, this is a finding.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000092-WSR-000055
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: Both the log file and Event Tracing for Windows (ETW) for each IIS 10.0 website must be enabled.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
Follow the procedures below for each site hosted on the IIS 10.0 web server:
Open the IIS 10.0 Manager.
Click the site name.
Click the "Logging" icon.
Under Log Event Destination, verify the "Both log file and ETW event" radio button is selected.
If the "Both log file and ETW event" radio button is not selected, this is a finding.
Note: "Microsoft-IIS-Logging/logs" must be enabled prior to configuring this setting. More configuration information is available at: https://blogs.intelink.gov/blogs/_disairrt/?p=1317

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000099-WSR-000061
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The IIS 10.0 website must produce log records that contain sufficient information to establish the outcome (success or failure) of IIS 10.0 website events.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
(W3C Is Enabled) Follow the procedures below for each site hosted on the IIS 10.0 web server:
Select the website being reviewed.
Under "IIS", double-click the "Logging" icon.
Verify the "Format:" under "Log File" is configured to "W3C".
Select "Fields".
Under "Custom Fields", verify the following fields are selected:
Request Header >> Connection
Request Header >> Warning
If any of the above fields are not selected, this is a finding.

(Custom Field - Request Header >> Connection) Follow the procedures below for each site hosted on the IIS 10.0 web server:
Select the website being reviewed.
Under "IIS", double-click the "Logging" icon.
Verify the "Format:" under "Log File" is configured to "W3C".
Select "Fields".
Under "Custom Fields", verify the following fields are selected:
Request Header >> Connection
Request Header >> Warning
If any of the above fields are not selected, this is a finding.

(Custom Field - Request Header >> Warning) Follow the procedures below for each site hosted on the IIS 10.0 web server:
Select the website being reviewed.
Under "IIS", double-click the "Logging" icon.
Verify the "Format:" under "Log File" is configured to "W3C".
Select "Fields".
Under "Custom Fields", verify the following fields are selected:
Request Header >> Connection
Request Header >> Warning
If any of the above fields are not selected, this is a finding.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000100-WSR-000064
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The IIS 10.0 website must produce log records containing sufficient information to establish the identity of any user/subject or process associated with an event.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
(W3C Is Enabled) Follow the procedures below for each site hosted on the IIS 10.0 web server:
Access the IIS 10.0 web server IIS 10.0 Manager.
Under "IIS", double-click the "Logging" icon.
Verify the "Format:" under "Log File" is configured to "W3C".
Select "Fields".
Under "Standard Fields", verify "User Agent", "User Name", and "Referrer" are selected.
Under "Custom Fields", verify the following fields have been configured:
Request Header >> Authorization
Response Header >> Content-Type
If any of the above fields are not selected, this is a finding.

(Standard Fields - User Agent) Follow the procedures below for each site hosted on the IIS 10.0 web server:
Access the IIS 10.0 web server IIS 10.0 Manager.
Under "IIS", double-click the "Logging" icon.
Verify the "Format:" under "Log File" is configured to "W3C".
Select "Fields".
Under "Standard Fields", verify "User Agent", "User Name", and "Referrer" are selected.
Under "Custom Fields", verify the following fields have been configured:
Request Header >> Authorization
Response Header >> Content-Type
If any of the above fields are not selected, this is a finding.

(Standard Fields - User Name) Follow the procedures below for each site hosted on the IIS 10.0 web server:
Access the IIS 10.0 web server IIS 10.0 Manager.
Under "IIS", double-click the "Logging" icon.
Verify the "Format:" under "Log File" is configured to "W3C".
Select "Fields".
Under "Standard Fields", verify "User Agent", "User Name", and "Referrer" are selected.
Under "Custom Fields", verify the following fields have been configured:
Request Header >> Authorization
Response Header >> Content-Type
If any of the above fields are not selected, this is a finding.

(Standard Fields - Referrer) Follow the procedures below for each site hosted on the IIS 10.0 web server:
Access the IIS 10.0 web server IIS 10.0 Manager.
Under "IIS", double-click the "Logging" icon.
Verify the "Format:" under "Log File" is configured to "W3C".
Select "Fields".
Under "Standard Fields", verify "User Agent", "User Name", and "Referrer" are selected.
Under "Custom Fields", verify the following fields have been configured:
Request Header >> Authorization
Response Header >> Content-Type
If any of the above fields are not selected, this is a finding.

(Custom Field - Request Header >> Authorization) Follow the procedures below for each site hosted on the IIS 10.0 web server:
Access the IIS 10.0 web server IIS 10.0 Manager.
Under "IIS", double-click the "Logging" icon.
Verify the "Format:" under "Log File" is configured to "W3C".
Select "Fields".
Under "Standard Fields", verify "User Agent", "User Name", and "Referrer" are selected.
Under "Custom Fields", verify the following fields have been configured:
Request Header >> Authorization
Response Header >> Content-Type
If any of the above fields are not selected, this is a finding.

(Custom Field - Response Header >> Content-Type) Follow the procedures below for each site hosted on the IIS 10.0 web server:
Access the IIS 10.0 web server IIS 10.0 Manager.
Under "IIS", double-click the "Logging" icon.
Verify the "Format:" under "Log File" is configured to "W3C".
Select "Fields".
Under "Standard Fields", verify "User Agent", "User Name", and "Referrer" are selected.
Under "Custom Fields", verify the following fields have been configured:
Request Header >> Authorization
Response Header >> Content-Type
If any of the above fields are not selected, this is a finding.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000141-WSR-000081
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The IIS 10.0 website must have Multipurpose Internet Mail Extensions (MIME) that invoke OS shell programs disabled.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
Follow the procedures below for each site hosted on the IIS 10.0 web server:
Open the IIS 10.0 Manager.
Click on the IIS 10.0 site.
Under IIS, double-click the “MIME Types” icon.
From the "Group by:" drop-down list, select "Content Type".
From the list of extensions under "Application", verify MIME types for OS shell program extensions have been removed, to include at a minimum, the following extensions:
.exe .dll .com .bat .csh
If any OS shell MIME types are configured, this is a finding.

Follow the procedures below for each site hosted on the IIS 10.0 web server:
Open the IIS 10.0 Manager.
Click on the IIS 10.0 site.
Under IIS, double-click the “MIME Types” icon.
From the "Group by:" drop-down list, select "Content Type".
From the list of extensions under "Application", verify MIME types for OS shell program extensions have been removed, to include at a minimum, the following extensions:
.exe .dll .com .bat .csh
If any OS shell MIME types are configured, this is a finding.

Follow the procedures below for each site hosted on the IIS 10.0 web server:
Open the IIS 10.0 Manager.
Click on the IIS 10.0 site.
Under IIS, double-click the “MIME Types” icon.
From the "Group by:" drop-down list, select "Content Type".
From the list of extensions under "Application", verify MIME types for OS shell program extensions have been removed, to include at a minimum, the following extensions:
.exe .dll .com .bat .csh
If any OS shell MIME types are configured, this is a finding.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000141-WSR-000085
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The IIS 10.0 website must have Web Distributed Authoring and Versioning (WebDAV) disabled.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
Follow the procedures below for each site hosted on the IIS 10.0 web server:
Open the IIS 10.0 Manager.
Select the IIS 10.0 website.
Review the features listed under the "IIS" section.
If the "WebDAV Authoring Rules" icon exists, this is a finding.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000142-WSR-000089
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: Each IIS 10.0 website must be assigned a default host header.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
Note: If the server being reviewed is hosting SharePoint, this is Not Applicable.
Follow the procedures below for each site hosted on the IIS 10.0 web server:
Open the IIS 10.0 Manager. Right-click on the site name under review. Select "Edit Bindings".
Verify there are hostname entries and unique IP addresses assigned to port 80 for HTTP and port 443 for HTTPS. Other approved and documented ports may be used.
If both hostname entries and unique IP addresses are not configured to port 80 for HTTP and port 443 for HTTPS (or other approved and documented port), this is a finding.
Note: If certificate handling is performed at the Proxy/Load Balancer, this is not a finding.
Note: If HTTP/Port 80 is not being used, and is not configured as above, this is not a finding.
Note: If this IIS 10.0 installation is supporting Microsoft Exchange, and not otherwise hosting any content, this requirement is Not Applicable.

Note: If the server being reviewed is hosting SharePoint, this is Not Applicable.
Follow the procedures below for each site hosted on the IIS 10.0 web server:
Open the IIS 10.0 Manager. Right-click on the site name under review. Select "Edit Bindings".
Verify there are hostname entries and unique IP addresses assigned to port 80 for HTTP and port 443 for HTTPS. Other approved and documented ports may be used.
If both hostname entries and unique IP addresses are not configured to port 80 for HTTP and port 443 for HTTPS (or other approved and documented port), this is a finding.
Note: If certificate handling is performed at the Proxy/Load Balancer, this is not a finding.
Note: If HTTP/Port 80 is not being used, and is not configured as above, this is not a finding.
Note: If this IIS 10.0 installation is supporting Microsoft Exchange, and not otherwise hosting any content, this requirement is Not Applicable.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000172-WSR-000104
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: A private IIS 10.0 website authentication mechanism must use client certificates to transmit session identifier to assure integrity.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
(SslRequireCert) Note: If the server being reviewed is a public IIS 10.0 web server, this is Not Applicable. Note: If the server being reviewed is hosting SharePoint, this is Not Applicable. Note: If the server being reviewed is hosting WSUS, this is Not Applicable. Note: If certificate handling is performed at the Proxy/Load Balancer, this is Not Applicable.
Follow the procedures below for each site hosted on the IIS 10.0 web server:
Open the IIS 10.0 Manager.
Double-click the 'SSL Settings' icon.
Verify the 'Clients Certificate Required' check box is selected.
If the 'Clients Certificate Required' check box is not selected, this is a finding.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000224-WSR-000136
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The IIS 10.0 website must generate unique session identifiers that cannot be reliably reproduced.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
Follow the procedures below for each site hosted on the IIS 10.0 web server:
Open the IIS 10.0 Manager.
Click the site name.
Under the "ASP.NET" section, select "Session State".
Under "Session State" Mode Settings, verify the "In Process" mode is selected.
If the "In Process" mode is selected, this is not a finding.
Alternative method:
Click the site name.
Select "Configuration Editor" under the "Management" section.
From the "Section:" drop-down list at the top of the configuration editor, locate "system.web/sessionState".
Verify the "mode" reflects "InProc".
If the "mode" is not set to "InProc", this is a finding.
If the system being reviewed is part of a Web Farm, interview the System Administrator to ensure Session State Tracking is enabled via a SQL server, or other means. If Session State Tracking is enabled on the Web Farm, this is not a finding.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000233-WSR-000146
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The IIS 10.0 website document directory must be in a separate partition from the IIS 10.0 websites system files.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
Follow the procedures below for each site hosted on the IIS 10.0 web server:
Open the IIS 10.0 Manager.
Click the site name under review.
Click the Advanced Settings from the Actions pane.
Change the Physical Path to the new partition and directory location. Note: If this IIS 10.0 installation is supporting Microsoft Exchange, and not otherwise hosting any content, this requirement is Not Applicable.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000246-WSR-000149
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The IIS 10.0 website must be configured to limit the maxURL.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
Follow the procedures below for each site hosted on the IIS 10.0 web server:
Open the IIS 10.0 Manager.
Click on the site name.
Double-click the "Request Filtering" icon.
Click "Edit Feature Settings" in the "Actions" pane.
If the "maxUrl" value is not set to "4096" or less, this is a finding.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000246-WSR-000149
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The IIS 10.0 website must be configured to limit the size of web requests.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
Follow the procedures below for each site hosted on the IIS 10.0 web server:
Open the IIS 10.0 Manager.
Click on the site name.
Double-click the "Request Filtering" icon.
Click "Edit Feature Settings" in the "Actions" pane.
If the "maxAllowedContentLength" value is not explicitly set to "30000000" or less or a length documented and approved by the ISSO, this is a finding.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000246-WSR-000149
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The IIS 10.0 websites Maximum Query String limit must be configured.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
Follow the procedures below for each site hosted on the IIS 10.0 web server:
Open the IIS 10.0 Manager.
Click the site name.
Double-click the "Request Filtering" icon.
Click “Edit Feature Settings” in the "Actions" pane.
If the "Maximum Query String" value is not set to "2048" or less, this is a finding.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000246-WSR-000149
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: Non-ASCII characters in URLs must be prohibited by any IIS 10.0 website.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
Follow the procedures below for each site hosted on the IIS 10.0 web server:
Open the IIS 10.0 Manager.
Click the site name.
Double-click the "Request Filtering" icon.
Click "Edit Feature Settings" in the "Actions" pane.
If the "Allow high-bit characters" check box is checked, this is a finding.
Note: If this IIS 10.0 installation is supporting Microsoft Exchange, and not otherwise hosting any content, this requirement is Not Applicable.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000246-WSR-000149
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: Double encoded URL requests must be prohibited by any IIS 10.0 website.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
Follow the procedures below for each site hosted on the IIS 10.0 web server:
Open the IIS 10.0 Manager.
Click the site name.
Double-click the "Request Filtering" icon.
Click "Edit Feature Settings" in the "Actions" pane.
If the "Allow double escaping" check box is checked, this is a finding.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000246-WSR-000149
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: Unlisted file extensions in URL requests must be filtered by any IIS 10.0 website.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
Note: If the server being reviewed is hosting SharePoint, this is Not Applicable.
Follow the procedures below for each site hosted on the IIS 10.0 web server:
Open the IIS 10.0 Manager.
Click the site name.
Double-click the 'Request Filtering' icon.
Click 'Edit Feature Settings' in the 'Actions' pane.
If the 'Allow unlisted file name extensions' check box is checked, this is a finding.
Note: If this IIS 10.0 installation is supporting Microsoft Exchange, and not otherwise hosting any content, this requirement is Not Applicable.
Note: If this IIS 10.0 installation is supporting Splunk, this requirement is Not Applicable.
Note: If this IIS 10.0 installation is supporting WSUS, this requirement is Not Applicable.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000251-WSR-000157
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: Directory Browsing on the IIS 10.0 website must be disabled.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
Follow the procedures below for each site hosted on the IIS 10.0 web server:
Click the Site.
Double-click the "Directory Browsing" icon.
If "Directory Browsing" is not installed, this is Not Applicable.
Under the "Actions" pane, verify "Directory Browsing" is "Disabled".
If "Directory Browsing" is not "Disabled", this is a finding.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000266-WSR-000159
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: Warning and error messages displayed to clients must be modified to minimize the identity of the IIS 10.0 website, patches, loaded modules, and directory paths.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
Follow the procedures below for each site hosted on the IIS 10.0 web server:
Open the IIS 10.0 Manager.
Click the site name under review.
Double-click the "Error Pages" icon.
Click each error message and click "Edit Feature" setting from the "Actions" pane.
If any error message is not set to "Detailed errors for local requests and custom error pages for remote requests" or "Custom error pages", this is a finding.

Follow the procedures below for each site hosted on the IIS 10.0 web server:
Open the IIS 10.0 Manager.
Click the site name under review.
Double-click the "Error Pages" icon.
Click each error message and click "Edit Feature" setting from the "Actions" pane.
If any error message is not set to "Detailed errors for local requests and custom error pages for remote requests" or "Custom error pages", this is a finding.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000266-WSR-000160
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: Debugging and trace information used to diagnose the IIS 10.0 website must be disabled.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
(Debug Set To False) Note: If the ".NET feature" is not installed, this check is Not Applicable.
Follow the procedures below for each site hosted on the IIS 10.0 web server:
Open the IIS 10.0 Manager.
Click the site name under review.
Double-click ".NET Compilation".
Scroll down to the "Behavior" section and verify the value for "Debug" is set to "False".
If the "Debug" value is not set to "False", this is a finding.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000295-WSR-000012
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The Idle Time-out monitor for each IIS 10.0 website must be enabled.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
If this IIS 10.0 installation is supporting Microsoft Exchange, and not otherwise hosting any content, this requirement is Not Applicable.
Follow the procedures below for each site hosted on the IIS 10.0 web server:
Open the IIS 10.0 Manager. Click the Application Pools. Highlight an Application Pool to review and click "Advanced Settings" in the "Actions" pane. Scroll down to the "Process Model" section and verify the value for "Idle Time-out" is not "0".
If the "Idle Time-out" is set to "0", this is a finding.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000295-WSR-000134
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The IIS 10.0 websites connection Timeout setting must be explicitly configured to disconnect an idle session.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
Follow the procedures below for each site hosted on the IIS 10.0 web server:
Open the IIS 10.0 Manager.
Click the site name.
Select "Configuration Editor" under the "Management" section.
From the "Section:" drop-down list at the top of the configuration editor, locate "system.web/sessionState".
Verify the "timeout" is set to "00:15:00 or less”.
If "timeout" is not set to "00:15:00 or less”, this is a finding.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000429-WSR-000113
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The IIS 10.0 private website must employ cryptographic mechanisms (TLS) and require client certificates.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
(SSL128 is enabled) Note: If SSL is installed on load balancer/proxy server through which traffic is routed to the IIS 10.0 server, and the IIS 10.0 server receives traffic from the load balancer/proxy server, the SSL requirement must be met on the load balancer/proxy server. In this case, this requirement is Not Applicable. Note: If this is a public-facing web server, this requirement is Not Applicable. Note: If this server is hosting WSUS, this requirement is Not Applicable. Note: If the server being reviewed is hosting SharePoint, this is Not Applicable.
Follow the procedures below for each site hosted on the IIS 10.0 web server:
Open the IIS 10.0 Manager.
Double-click the "SSL Settings" icon under the "IIS" section.
Verify "Require SSL" is checked.
Verify "Client Certificates Required" is selected.
Click the site under review.
Select "Configuration Editor" under the "Management" section.
From the "Section:" drop-down list at the top of the configuration editor, locate "system.webServer/security/access".
The value for "sslFlags" set must include "ssl128".
If the "Require SSL" is not selected, this is a finding. If the "Client Certificates Required" is not selected, this is a finding. If the "sslFlags" is not set to "ssl128", this is a finding.

(SslRequireCert) Note: If SSL is installed on load balancer/proxy server through which traffic is routed to the IIS 10.0 server, and the IIS 10.0 server receives traffic from the load balancer/proxy server, the SSL requirement must be met on the load balancer/proxy server. In this case, this requirement is Not Applicable. Note: If this is a public-facing web server, this requirement is Not Applicable. Note: If this server is hosting WSUS, this requirement is Not Applicable. Note: If the server being reviewed is hosting SharePoint, this is Not Applicable.
Follow the procedures below for each site hosted on the IIS 10.0 web server:
Open the IIS 10.0 Manager.
Double-click the "SSL Settings" icon under the "IIS" section.
Verify "Require SSL" is checked.
Verify "Client Certificates Required" is selected.
Click the site under review.
Select "Configuration Editor" under the "Management" section.
From the "Section:" drop-down list at the top of the configuration editor, locate "system.webServer/security/access".
The value for "sslFlags" set must include "ssl128".
If the "Require SSL" is not selected, this is a finding. If the "Client Certificates Required" is not selected, this is a finding. If the "sslFlags" is not set to "ssl128", this is a finding.

(Require SSL is enabled) Note: If SSL is installed on load balancer/proxy server through which traffic is routed to the IIS 10.0 server, and the IIS 10.0 server receives traffic from the load balancer/proxy server, the SSL requirement must be met on the load balancer/proxy server. In this case, this requirement is Not Applicable. Note: If this is a public-facing web server, this requirement is Not Applicable. Note: If this server is hosting WSUS, this requirement is Not Applicable. Note: If the server being reviewed is hosting SharePoint, this is Not Applicable.
Follow the procedures below for each site hosted on the IIS 10.0 web server:
Open the IIS 10.0 Manager.
Double-click the "SSL Settings" icon under the "IIS" section.
Verify "Require SSL" is checked.
Verify "Client Certificates Required" is selected.
Click the site under review.
Select "Configuration Editor" under the "Management" section.
From the "Section:" drop-down list at the top of the configuration editor, locate "system.webServer/security/access".
The value for "sslFlags" set must include "ssl128".
If the "Require SSL" is not selected, this is a finding. If the "Client Certificates Required" is not selected, this is a finding. If the "sslFlags" is not set to "ssl128", this is a finding.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000439-WSR-000152
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: IIS 10.0 website session IDs must be sent to the client using TLS.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
Follow the procedures below for each site hosted on the IIS 10.0 web server:
Access the IIS 10.0 Manager.
Select the website being reviewed.
Under "Management" section, double-click the "Configuration Editor" icon.
From the "Section:" drop-down list, select "system.webServer/asp".
Expand the "session" section.
Verify the "keepSessionIdSecure" is set to "True".
If the "keepSessionIdSecure" is not set to "True", this is a finding.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000439-WSR-000154
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The IIS 10.0 website must have a unique application pool.

oval-def:criterion.comment: system.web/httpCookies:require SSL is set to True
system.web/sessionState:compressionEnabled is set to False

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000516-WSR-000174
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The IIS 10.0 website must have a unique application pool.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
Note: If the IIS Application Pool is hosting Microsoft SharePoint, this is Not Applicable.
If this IIS 10.0 installation is supporting Microsoft Exchange, and not otherwise hosting any content, this requirement is Not Applicable.
Open the IIS 10.0 Manager.
Click "Application Pools".
In the list of Application Pools, review the "Applications" column and verify unique application pools for each website.
If any Application Pools are being used for more than one website, this is a finding.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000516-WSR-000174
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The maximum number of requests an application pool can process for each IIS 10.0 website must be explicitly set.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
Note: If the IIS Application Pool is hosting Microsoft SharePoint, this is Not Applicable.
If this IIS 10.0 installation is supporting Microsoft Exchange, and not otherwise hosting any content, this requirement is Not Applicable.
Open the IIS 10.0 Manager.
Perform for each Application Pool.
Click "Application Pools".
Highlight an Application Pool and click "Advanced Settings" in the "Action" Pane.
Scroll down to the "Recycling section" and verify the value for "Request Limit" is set to a value other than "0".
If the "Request Limit" is set to a value of "0", this is a finding.
If the system must require "Request Limit" to be set to "0", it is documented and approved by the ISSO, this is not a finding.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000516-WSR-000174
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The application pool for each IIS 10.0 website must have a recycle time explicitly set.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
(Regular time interval option) Note: If the IIS Application Pool is hosting Microsoft SharePoint, this is Not Applicable. Note: If the IIS Application Pool is hosting Microsoft Exchange and not otherwise hosting any content, this is Not Applicable.
Open the IIS 10.0 Manager.
Expand "Application Pools".
Perform the following for each Application Pool:
Highlight an Application Pool and click "Recycling" in the "Actions" pane.
In the Recycling Conditions window, verify at least one condition is checked as desired by the organization (e.g., Regular Time Intervals, Scheduled Time).
If no conditions are checked, this is a finding.
Click "Next".
In the Recycling Events to Log window, verify at minimum the Recycling Events are selected that correspond to the conditions defined in the previous step (e.g., Regular Time Intervals, Scheduled Time).
If no events are selected, this is a finding.
Click "Cancel".

(Specific time option) Note: If the IIS Application Pool is hosting Microsoft SharePoint, this is Not Applicable. Note: If the IIS Application Pool is hosting Microsoft Exchange and not otherwise hosting any content, this is Not Applicable.
Open the IIS 10.0 Manager.
Expand "Application Pools".
Perform the following for each Application Pool:
Highlight an Application Pool and click "Recycling" in the "Actions" pane.
In the Recycling Conditions window, verify at least one condition is checked as desired by the organization (e.g., Regular Time Intervals, Scheduled Time).
If no conditions are checked, this is a finding.
Click "Next".
In the Recycling Events to Log window, verify at minimum the Recycling Events are selected that correspond to the conditions defined in the previous step (e.g., Regular Time Intervals, Scheduled Time).
If no events are selected, this is a finding.
Click "Cancel".

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000516-WSR-000174
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The application pools rapid fail protection for each IIS 10.0 website must be enabled.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
Note: If the IIS Application Pool is hosting Microsoft SharePoint, this is Not Applicable.
If this IIS 10.0 installation is supporting Microsoft Exchange, and not otherwise hosting any content, this requirement is Not Applicable.
Open the IIS 10.0 Manager.
Click "Application Pools".
Perform the following for each Application Pool:
Highlight an Application Pool to review and click "Advanced Settings" in the "Actions" pane.
Scroll down to the "Rapid Fail Protection" section and verify the value for "Enabled" is set to "True".
If the "Rapid Fail Protection:Enabled" is not set to "True", this is a finding.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000516-WSR-000174
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: The application pools rapid fail protection settings for each IIS 10.0 website must be managed.

oval-def:criterion.comment:
Note: If the IIS Application Pool is hosting Microsoft SharePoint, this is Not Applicable.
If this IIS 10.0 installation is supporting Microsoft Exchange, and not otherwise hosting any content, this requirement is Not Applicable.
Open the IIS 10.0 Manager.
Click "Application Pools".
Perform the following for each Application Pool:
Highlight an Application Pool to review and click "Advanced Settings" in the "Actions" pane.
Scroll down to the "Rapid Fail Protection" section and verify the value for "Failure Interval" is set to "5".
If the "Failure Interval" is not set to "5" or less, this is a finding.

oval-def:metadata.oval-def:title: SRG-APP-000141-WSR-000087
oval-def:metadata.oval-def:description: Backup interactive scripts on the IIS 10.0 server must be removed.

oval-def:criterion.comment: Search Web Directory for the listed script extensions Search for the following files: *.bak, *.old, *.temp, *.tmp, *.backup, or “copy of...”. If files with these extensions are found, this is a finding.
.NET Framework 4.5 Features Installed?
.NET Framework 3.5 Features Installed?

Microsoft WSUS Installed?
Splunk Installed?
ASP.NET Installed?
CGI Utilization
NDES Installation

SSL is enabled - Click the site name. Double-click the 'SSL Settings' icon. Verify 'Require SSL is selected' If the 'Require SSL ' is not selected, this is a finding.

win-def:appcmd_test.comment: Both the log file and Event Tracing for Windows (ETW) for each IIS 10.0 website must be enabled.

Remove MIME Type: dll, com, bat, csh

win-def:appcmd_test.comment: Verify there exists a hostname entries and unique IP addresses to port 80 for HTTP.
Verify there exists a hostname entries and unique IP addresses to port 443 for HTTPS.

win-def:appcmdlistconfig_test.comment: SSL128 is enabled - Verify Require SSL is checked. Verify Client Certificates Required is selected. Click the site under review. Select Configuration Editor under the Management section. From the Section: drop-down list at the top of the configuration editor, locate “system.webServer/security/access”. The values for sslFlags must include ssl128. If the Require SSL is not selected, this is a finding. If the Client Certificates Required is not selected, this is a finding. If the sslFlags is not set to ssl128, this is a finding.

SslRequireCert Setting - Verify Require SSL is checked. Verify Client Certificates Required is selected. Click the site under review. Select Configuration Editor under the Management section. From the Section: drop-down list at the top of the configuration editor, locate “system.webServer/security/access”. The values for sslFlags must include ssl128. If the Require SSL is not selected, this is a finding. If the Client Certificates Required is not selected, this is a finding. If the sslFlags is not set to ssl128, this is a finding.
The IIS 10.0 website session state must be enabled.
